slides.title: Passing Information 📬
slides.content:
Information can be passed to functions as a parameter. Parameters act as variables inside the function.

You can add as many parameters as you want, just separate them with a comma `,`.

slides.title: Defining Parameters
slides.content: A function that takes a name and prints a greeting.

quizData.question: What is the output of greet("Jenny");?
quizData.explanation: We passed the string argument 'Jenny' into the 'name' parameter, so it outputs 'Hello Jenny!'.

slides.title: Multiple Parameters 👨‍👩‍👧
slides.content:
Inside the function, you must declare both the **type** and the **name** for each parameter.

`void getBio(string name, int age) { ... }`

slides.title: Example

quizData.question: If a function demands two parameters, you MUST provide exactly two arguments when you call it.
quizData.explanation: Yes. If the function expects two things (a string and an int), and you only pass one (or pass none), you will get a compiling error.
